title: Crispy Venezuelan Vanilla Crumble
description: A funky, crumbly, energizing Venezuelan dessert featuring vanilla pod, grilled fruit, and a nut‑free crumble topping.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Slice mangoes and plantains into 1/4-inch thick rounds and set aside.
Split the vanilla pod lengthwise and scrape the seeds into a bowl, discarding the pod.
In a small bowl, whisk together flour, oats, powdered sugar, and sea salt until uniform.
Stir in melted coconut oil and brown sugar to form coarse crumbs.
Brush fruit slices lightly with melted coconut oil, then sprinkle half of the crumb mixture over them.
Place fruit on the grill and cook 3-4 minutes per side until grill marks appear and fruit is caramelized.
In a separate non‑stick skillet, toast the remaining crumb mixture over low heat until golden and fragrant.
Once the fruit is grilled, top each serving with a spoonful of the toasted crumb mixture.
Finish by sprinkling a pinch of cinnamon over the crumble and serve warm immediately.
